source_jobs.title: Market Manager
source_jobs.description:
About 8Fleet:

8Fleet is hiring! 8Fleet is a rapidly growing LA-based technology and logistics startup building the operational infrastructure for both autonomous and non-autonomous rideshare fleets. With our anticipated growth, we’re searching for a passionate Market Manager to join and grow with our team. You’ll be involved in every aspect of operations as we expand rapidly!

About the Role:

The Market Manager is responsible for the management of 8Fleet's operations in Los Angeles, including but not limited to: managing end-to-end operations, strategic planning, data oversight, business operations, and BD. This is an essential role that requires operational expertise, a deep understanding of the ridesharing industry, and a founder’s mindset.

This role will tackle key business priorities that amplify the growth of our business and facilitate operational excellence. Using strong operational and analytical experience from past environments, this person will tackle key projects that will be instrumental in driving our business forward. We need a strategic critical thinker who can start at 20,000 feet and then dive deep into the details to ensure high quality results.

The ideal candidate will have a “doer” attitude, experience in operations, and be comfortable with fast-paced decision-making, have displayed leadership, and can provide open communication. This individual will be critical in helping navigate and grow the business, offering valuable insights and advice on strategic decision-making, implementation, and execution. We’re looking for someone who can successfully execute the role and grow within the Company.

You are driven. You are endlessly curious. You are passionate about finding creative solutions to challenges.

Key Responsibilities:

 - Fleet Growth & Management:
   
   - Own all financial and operational metrics to ensure optimal revenue and profit maximization
   
   - Oversee the day-to-day operations of the fleet, including driver scheduling, dispatch, and performance monitoring.
   
   - Manage vehicle maintenance and repair programs to ensure optimal fleet utilization and safety.
   
   - Partner with the Growth team to develop and execute strategies to recruit, onboard, and retain a high-performing driver base.

 - Financial Performance:
   
   - Analyze fleet performance data to identify areas for improvement in profitability and operational efficiency.
   
   - Develop and implement cost-effective strategies to maximize fleet profitability.
   
   - Monitor key performance indicators (KPIs) and track progress towards achieving business goals.

 - Driver Support & Training:
   
   - Provide ongoing support and training to drivers on industry best practices, safety regulations, and customer service.
   
   - Foster a positive and supportive environment for all employees.

 - Compliance & Safety:
   
   - Ensure compliance with all relevant regulations and safety standards.
   
   - Conduct regular safety audits and implement necessary corrective actions.

Successful Candidates Will Bring:

 - 2+ years of operations management, business consulting, or related experience managing operations, building and developing teams, optimizing processes, and delivering business goals

 - 2+ years of people management experience, high EQ, and proven ability to deliver outstanding results through others in a timely manner using a strong sense of focus, organization, and meticulous attention to detail

 - Goal focus with a strong bias toward action

 - Entrepreneurial hustle and proven self-starter with owner’s mentality; some entrepreneurial experience is preferred

 - Data-driven mindset, strong business judgment, and proven ability to perform analysis, draw key insights, and form and execute a strategy

 - Strong communication and interpersonal skills to build relationships, manage a team, influence stakeholders, and inspire others

 - Excellent organization and time management

 - Willingness to get your hands dirty

source_jobs.title: Fleet Specialist (Overnight Operations)
source_jobs.description:
SCHEDULE: SUNDAY–THURSDAY, 10:00 PM – 6:00 AM




ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re looking for a hands-on, detail-oriented Fleet Specialist to join our overnight operations team. You’ll play a critical role in keeping our rideshare fleet clean, safe, and ready to roll every morning. This position bridges the gap between our PM and AM operations, ensuring every vehicle is properly received, maintained, and launched without downtime.

This is a physical, fast-paced role ideal for someone who takes pride in keeping vehicles running smoothly and enjoys problem-solving in a dynamic environment.



WHAT YOU’LL DO

End-of-Shift Support (PM Close)

 - Receive returning vehicles at the end of the day and collect keys, tablets, and other driver equipment.

 - Note any reported issues, and inspect for damage or warning lights.

 - Perform light interior cleaning (vacuuming, wipe-downs) and restock basic supplies.

Preventative Maintenance & Vehicle Upkeep

 - Conduct basic maintenance including oil and filter changes, tire pressure checks, and fluid top-offs (coolant, wiper fluid, etc.).

 - Replace wipers, bulbs, and other minor components as needed.

 - Log maintenance tasks and communicate larger repairs to the Fleet Manager or vendors.

Fleet Readiness & Cleaning

 - Pressure wash vehicles for the next shift.

 - Maintain garage cleanliness, tool organization, and inventory of supplies.

Morning Launch Support (AM Start)

 - Prepare vehicles for dispatch before drivers arrive.

 - Troubleshoot basic app, phone, or equipment issues to ensure on-time launches.

Administrative Support

 - Track vehicle assignments, inspections, and maintenance logs.

 - Report and document incidents, driver notes, or missing items.

 - Support the operations team with ad-hoc fleet or logistics tasks.


WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - High school diploma or equivalent required.

 - Background in automotive service, fleet operations, or logistics strongly preferred.

 - Basic mechanical knowledge — comfortable changing oil, checking tires, and performing minor repairs.

 - Exceptional attention to detail and organizational skills.

 - Strong communication and teamwork, with the ability to work independently overnight.

 - Comfortable using mobile apps and learning new digital tools.

 - Must be able to lift 50 lbs, stand for extended periods, and work in a garage environment.

 - Valid driver’s license with a clean driving record required.

Compensation:

 - $24/hour

Equal Opportunity Employer:

8Fleet is an equal opportunity employer and values diversity at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.

Disclaimer:

This job description may be updated at any time without prior notice.

source_jobs.title: Fleet Specialist (Part-Time)
source_jobs.description:
Job Title: Fleet Specialist

Location: Hub - LA

Reporting to: Market Manager



About 8Fleet:

8Fleet is hiring! 8Fleet is a rapidly growing LA-based technology and logistics startup building the operational infrastructure for both non-autonomous and future autonomous rideshare fleets. With our anticipated growth, we’re searching for passionate team members to join and grow with our team. You’ll be involved in every aspect of the operations as we rapidly expand across Los Angeles and beyond!

About the Role:

We are looking for a highly organized and reliable Fleet Specialist to join our team. In this role, you'll be a key part of our daily operations, ensuring our fleet of vehicles is ready for our drivers at the start of each shift and accounted for at the end. You'll be the point person for all things related to vehicle access and readiness, helping to maintain a smooth and efficient workflow for the entire team. This is a hands-on position that requires strong attention to detail and a proactive approach to problem-solving.

What You'll Do:

 - Vehicle and Key Management: Distribute and collect assigned vehicle keys and other necessary equipment at the start and end of each shift.

 - Fleet Readiness: Assist with basic cleaning and staging of vehicles to ensure they are ready for use.

 - Vehicle Inspections: Conduct thorough pre- and post-ride inspections to identify and report any damage, mechanical issues, retrieve and log any lost or missing items.

 - Technical Support: Troubleshoot basic issues with fleet applications and other in-vehicle technology, escalating more complex problems as needed.

 - Reporting: Maintain accurate records of key assignments, vehicle condition, and shift activity.

 - General Support: Provide administrative and logistical support to the fleet management team as needed.

What You'll Bring to the Table:

 - A high school diploma or equivalent is required. Previous experience in a logistics, warehouse, or fleet-related role is a plus.

 - Exceptional attention to detail and a strong ability to manage multiple tasks and responsibilities at once.

 - Excellent verbal communication skills and a professional, positive attitude.

 - Basic knowledge of mobile applications and the ability to learn new software quickly.

 - Physical Requirements: Ability to stand for extended periods, as well as lift, bend, and move around a vehicle yard. Must be able to pass a background check.

Equal Opportunity Employer:

8Fleet is an equal opportunity employer and values diversity at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.

Disclaimer:

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer (Full-Stack)
source_jobs.description:
Job Title: Senior Software Engineer (Full-Stack)

Location: Los Angeles; Open to Remote

Reporting to: President; Co-Founder

About 8Fleet:

8Fleet is hiring! 8Fleet is a rapidly growing LA-based technology and logistics startup building the operational infrastructure for both non-autonomous and future autonomous rideshare fleets. With our anticipated growth, we’re searching for passionate team members to join and grow with our team. You’ll be involved in every aspect of the operations as we rapidly expand across Los Angeles and beyond!

About the Role:

We are seeking a seasoned and versatile Senior Full-Stack Software Engineer to own critical features across our entire technology stack, from the database and core APIs to the client-side user experience. This role demands an engineer who thrives in a fast-paced environment, can take ambiguous requirements and turn them into robust, high-quality, and scalable solutions, and can mentor peers in best practices.

What You'll Do:


APPLICATION DEVELOPMENT & OWNERSHIP

 - Design, develop, and deploy new features and services across the full stack—including our fleet management dashboards, internal operations tools, and core logistics APIs.

 - Own the complete lifecycle of features, from technical design and planning through implementation, testing, and production deployment.

 - Write clean, maintainable, and well-tested code in both frontend and backend environments, ensuring performance and security are prioritized.

 - Collaborate closely with Product Managers and Designers to translate business requirements and mockups into seamless, high-performance user interfaces.


ARCHITECTURE & SCALABILITY

 - Lead technical design discussions and contribute significantly to the architecture of new systems, focusing on scalability to support 8Fleet’s growing volume of users and vehicles.

 - Identify and address performance bottlenecks across the application stack, focusing on database query optimization and API latency reduction.

 - Implement robust monitoring, logging, and alerting for production systems to ensure high availability and reliability.

 - Advocate for and implement engineering best practices, including continuous integration/continuous deployment (CI/CD) pipelines, code reviews, and automated testing strategies.


CROSS-FUNCTIONAL COLLABORATION & MENTORSHIP

 - Work effectively across engineering teams (Data, DevOps, Mobile) to integrate systems and ensure seamless data flow.

 - Mentor and coach mid-level and junior engineers, fostering a collaborative environment and elevating the team's overall technical capabilities.

 - 

What You'll Bring to the Table:

 - 5+ years of progressive experience in software engineering, with significant experience operating as a Full-Stack Engineer.

 - Expert proficiency in a modern backend language (e.g., Python, Go, Node.js) and experience designing and implementing highly available RESTful or GraphQL APIs.

 - Deep proficiency in a modern frontend framework (e.g., React, Vue, or Angular), with a focus on building complex, stateful web applications.

 - Proven experience with relational and/or NoSQL databases (e.g., PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB) and advanced SQL query optimization.

 - Strong understanding of cloud services (AWS, GCP, or Azure) and experience deploying and managing applications within a cloud environment (ideally using containerization like Docker/Kubernetes).

 - Demonstrated ability to navigate the entire stack, from infrastructure to data modeling to presentation layer.

 - Exceptional problem-solving skills and a strong commitment to code quality, testing, and documentation.

Bonus Points For:

 - Experience with geographical data, mapping services (e.g., Google Maps API, Mapbox), or real-time tracking systems.

 - Experience in the logistics, rideshare, or mobility-as-a-service (MaaS) industries.

 - Familiarity with microservices architecture and event-driven patterns (e.g., Kafka).

source_jobs.title: Driver Growth Associate
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT 8FLEET

8Fleet is hiring! 8Fleet is a rapidly growing LA-based technology and logistics startup building the operational infrastructure for both autonomous and non-autonomous rideshare fleets. As we scale quickly, we’re looking for operators who thrive in ambiguity, care deeply about performance, and aren’t afraid to get their hands dirty.



ABOUT THE ROLE

8Fleet is hiring a Driver Growth Associate to help build and improve how we attract and onboard drivers. This is not a traditional recruiting role. You won’t be interviewing drivers all day. Instead, you’ll focus on improving the systems and processes that bring drivers into 8Fleet — from where they first hear about us to when they complete onboarding and start driving.

You’ll work closely with the Head of Growth and the team responsible for driver outreach and coordination. Your goal is to make our driver growth efforts more effective, more predictable, and easier to operate.

This role is ideal for someone who enjoys improving processes, testing ideas, and seeing real-world results from their work.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Help run and improve driver acquisition across channels like job boards, social media, referrals, and local partnerships

 - Turn driver demand into clear weekly and monthly goals, and help build plans to consistently meet them

 - Test and improve messaging, incentives, and outreach to increase driver interest and follow-through

 - Track how drivers move through the onboarding process and identify where people get stuck or drop off

 - Work with Operations to remove unnecessary steps, clarify expectations, and speed up onboarding

 - Provide clear guidance and structure to the team handling driver outreach and follow-ups

 - Help build repeatable processes so driver growth is less reactive and more predictable

 - Monitor early driver success and retention and use those insights to improve future hiring efforts


SUCCESSFUL CANDIDATES WILL BRING

 - 1–4+ years of experience in growth, operations, recruiting operations, marketing, or a similar role

 - Comfort working with basic metrics and tracking progress across a process or funnel

 - A practical, hands-on mindset — you like improving how things work

 - Ability to collaborate with different teams and keep projects moving forward

 - Clear communication skills and attention to detail


NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience with job boards, candidate outreach, or local lead generation

 - Familiarity with basic CRM, ATS, or tracking tools

 - Experience in logistics, gig work, or service-based businesses


COMPENSATION

 - $70k-80k

 - Bonus eligibility and equity package


EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER

8Fleet is an equal opportunity employer and values diversity at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.



DISCLAIMER

This job description may be updated at any time without prior notice.

source_jobs.title: Driver Operations Manager
source_jobs.description:
About 8Fleet:

8Fleet is hiring! 8Fleet is a rapidly growing LA-based technology and logistics startup building the operational infrastructure for both autonomous and non-autonomous rideshare fleets. With our anticipated growth, we’re searching for a passionate Market Manager to join and grow with our team. You’ll be involved in every aspect of operations as we expand rapidly!

About the Role:

The Driver Operations Manager is responsible for owning and improving driver performance within 8Fleet’s Los Angeles market. This role is fully focused on analyzing driver data, identifying performance gaps, and turning insights into concrete actions executed by Ops Coordinators on the ground.

You will serve as the connective tissue between driver performance data and the Ops Coordinators who work directly with drivers. This role ensures that what the data says actually shows up in day-to-day conversations, coaching, and execution in the field.

This is a hands-on role for someone who is equally comfortable deep in dashboards and performance analyses as they are rolling up their sleeves to solve real operational problems. The ideal candidate is an analytics powerhouse with a strong operator’s mindset — someone who will do whatever it takes to improve outcomes, including changing a tire if needed.

You are driven. You are curious. You care deeply about performance — and you take pride in execution.

Key Responsibilities:

Driver Performance Analytics & Insights

 - Own driver-level performance metrics including utilization, online hours, trip quality, acceptance rates, cancellations, reliability, and safety indicators

 - Build, maintain, and improve dashboards and reporting to surface trends and identify underperformance.

 - Analyze driver behavior and performance patterns to identify root causes and improvement opportunities.

 - Clearly translate data insights into actionable recommendations for Ops Coordinators.

Execution Through Ops Coordinators

 - Partner closely with Ops Coordinators to ensure performance insights are effectively communicated to drivers

 - Equip Coordinators with clear talking points, priorities, and follow-ups rooted in data

 - Support and reinforce performance conversations through coaching frameworks and escalation paths

 - Track execution quality and follow-through to ensure data-driven initiatives actually move metrics

On-the-Ground Operational Support

 - Stay closely connected to day-to-day fleet operations to understand real-world constraints behind the data

 - Step in to support operational issues as needed, including driver or vehicle-related problems

 - Lead by example with a hands-on approach to problem-solving and execution

Continuous Improvement & Feedback Loop

 - Monitor the impact of performance initiatives and iterate quickly based on results

 - Surface insights from Ops Coordinators and drivers to refine analytics, targets, and processes

 - Act as a feedback loop between field execution and performance reporting

Successful Candidates Will Bring

 - 2+ years of experience in operations, analytics, logistics, or a similar execution-heavy role

 - Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret data, identify patterns, and drive action

 - Experience working with performance metrics, dashboards, and reporting

 - Ability to translate data into clear, actionable guidance for frontline teams

 - Strong ownership mentality and bias toward action

 - Willingness to roll up your sleeves and support operations as needed

 - Strong communication and collaboration skills across analytical and operational teams

 - Excellent organization and time management in a fast-paced environment

 - Experience with rideshare platforms (Uber, Lyft) or fleet operations is a strong plus

Available full-time, on-site in Los Angeles

Job Title: Operations Coordinator

Location: Hub - SF

Reporting to: General Manager



About 8Fleet:

8Fleet is hiring! 8Fleet is a rapidly growing LA-based technology and logistics startup building the operational infrastructure for both non-autonomous and future autonomous rideshare fleets. With our anticipated growth, we’re searching for passionate team members to join and grow with our team. You’ll be involved in every aspect of the operations as we rapidly expand across Los Angeles and beyond!

About the Role:

We are looking for a highly organized and reliable Operations Coordinator to join our team. In this role, you'll be a key part of our daily operations, interfacing with drivers and ensuring our fleet of vehicles is ready for our drivers at the start of each shift and accounted for at the end. You'll be the point person for managing shifts, helping to maintain a smooth and efficient workflow for the entire team. This is a hands-on position that requires strong attention to detail and a proactive approach to problem-solving.

What You'll Do:

 - Supporting Drivers: Providing assistance and support to drivers, including troubleshooting issues, on-road coaching, and managing overall performance.

 - Scheduling Maintenance and Repairs: Coordinating routine maintenance, inspections, and repairs to ensure vehicles are in good working order.

 - Managing Inventory: Maintaining records of keys, vehicle equipment, and other supplies needed for fleet operations.

 - Monitoring Fuel Levels and Other Key Metrics: Tracking fuel consumption and other relevant data to optimize fleet performance.

 - Ensuring Compliance: Adhering to safety regulations, DOT regulations, and other relevant standards.

 - Optimizing Routes and Logistics: Working to improve routing, scheduling, and other aspects of fleet operations to maximize efficiency and minimize costs.

 - Working with External Vendors: Coordinating with mechanics, repair shops, and other vendors to ensure timely and cost-effective repairs.

 - Maintaining Records: Keeping accurate records of vehicle maintenance, repairs, and other relevant data.

 - Assisting with vehicle setup.

 - Process Improvement: Identifying and implementing process improvements to enhance fleet operations.

 - Scheduling and Time & Attendance Tracking: Monitoring, managing, editing, and approving timecards as well as creating weekly schedules for all supervisors and drivers.

Successful Candidates Will Bring:

 - 1-3 years of experience in startups or logistics.

 - Strong problem-solving skills and ability to multi-task under pressure.

 - Excellent communication, interpersonal, and leadership skills.

 - Proficiency in Microsoft Office a plus!

 - Bachelor's degree required.

 - Experience with ridesharing platforms (Uber, Lyft) is a strong plus.

 - Must be comfortable working early morning or late night hours

Compensation:

 - Pay: $70,000 – $80,000
   The pay listed is the salary range for this position. Any specific offer will vary based on the successful applicant’s education, experience, skills, abilities, geographic location, and alignment with market data.

ABOUT 8FLEET

8Fleet is hiring! We are a rapidly growing LA-based technology and logistics startup building the operational infrastructure for both autonomous and non-autonomous rideshare fleets. With our anticipated growth, we are searching for driven, passionate team members to join our People team. You will be involved in every aspect of our operations as we rapidly expand across Los Angeles and beyond, playing a pivotal role in keeping our people operations running smoothly as we scale.


ABOUT THE ROLE

We are seeking an LA-based HR Specialist to serve as a key player in our growing organization. This role sits at the center of our People Operations, with a primary focus on managing complex payroll operations and benefits administration for a dynamic, high-volume frontline workforce.

In addition to being our go-to expert for accurate and compliant payroll and benefits processing, you will wear a generalist hat, stepping in to support adhoc HR functions such as onboarding, employee relations, and core compliance. If you are detail-oriented, highly analytical, process-driven, and comfortable in a fast-paced startup environment, you will thrive here.


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES


PAYROLL AND COMPLIANCE

 - End-to-End Processing: Oversee and manage the weekly payroll process for a high volume of non-exempt employees in California, validating hours, overtime, differentials, and PTO.

 - Exempt Payroll: Reconcile and approve semi-monthly payroll for exempt employees in partnership with our PEO, managing reimbursement processes as needed.

 - Compliance Expert: Serve as the subject matter expert on California state wage and hour laws, proactively monitoring changes and ensuring deep adherence.

 - Issue Resolution: Partner with operations managers to resolve timekeeping discrepancies and proactively identify/resolve complex payroll data issues.

 - Tax & Deductions: Manage the preparation and submission of payroll tax filings, and oversee employee deductions, complex benefit deductions, and garnishments.


BENEFITS ADMINISTRATION

 - Program Management: Administer all employee benefit programs (medical, dental, vision, 401(k), life insurance, FSA/HSA), processing accurate enrollments, changes, and terminations.

 - Employee Support: Serve as the primary point of contact for escalated benefits and payroll inquiries, assisting employees with claims and liaising with brokers and carriers.

 - Open Enrollment: Manage annual open enrollment from initial planning and communication to system configuration and deduction audits.

 - Audits & Integrations: Ensure seamless integration between HRIS/Payroll and vendor platforms. Lead internal and external audits (e.g., 401(k), Workers' Comp).


ONBOARDING/EMPLOYEE RELATIONS/HRIS ADMINISTRATION

 - Onboarding & Offboarding: Facilitate new hire paperwork, conduct HR orientations, and coordinate offboarding procedures (exit interviews, final paperwork).

 - Employee Relations & Support: Provide guidance on HR policies, assist the HR Manager with resolving tier-1 employee relations issues, and support engagement initiatives.

 - HR Administration: Maintain clean data across HRIS, timekeeping, and PEO systems. Process routine HR paperwork (new hires, job changes).

 - Talent Acquisition & Performance: Occasionally assist with background checks, onboarding, and supporting managers with performance documentation.


QUALIFICATIONS

 - 1-2+ years of HR/People Operations experience.

 - Bachelor’s Degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, or Business Administration.

 - Strong attention to detail, analytical skills, and comfort handling highly sensitive employee data.

 - A low-ego, service-oriented mindset with the agility to pivot between high-level payroll strategy and routine HR administrative tasks.

 - Clear, friendly communication skills with the ability to build trust quickly across all levels of the organization.


COMPENSATION

 - Base Salary Range: $65,000 to $75,000 plus equity.

source_jobs.title: Fleet Specialist (Overnight Operations)
source_jobs.description:
SCHEDULE: SUNDAY–THURSDAY, 10:00 PM – 6:00 AM




ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re looking for a hands-on, detail-oriented Fleet Specialist to join our overnight operations team. You’ll play a critical role in keeping our rideshare fleet clean, safe, and ready to roll every morning. This position bridges the gap between our PM and AM operations, ensuring every vehicle is properly received, maintained, and launched without downtime.

This is a physical, fast-paced role ideal for someone who takes pride in keeping vehicles running smoothly and enjoys problem-solving in a dynamic environment.



WHAT YOU’LL DO

End-of-Shift Support (PM Close)

 - Receive returning vehicles at the end of the day and collect keys, tablets, and other driver equipment.

 - Note any reported issues, and inspect for damage or warning lights.

 - Perform light interior cleaning (vacuuming, wipe-downs) and restock basic supplies.

Preventative Maintenance & Vehicle Upkeep

 - Conduct basic maintenance including oil and filter changes, tire pressure checks, and fluid top-offs (coolant, wiper fluid, etc.).

 - Replace wipers, bulbs, and other minor components as needed.

 - Log maintenance tasks and communicate larger repairs to the Fleet Manager or vendors.

Fleet Readiness & Cleaning

 - Pressure wash vehicles for the next shift.

 - Maintain garage cleanliness, tool organization, and inventory of supplies.

Morning Launch Support (AM Start)

 - Prepare vehicles for dispatch before drivers arrive.

 - Troubleshoot basic app, phone, or equipment issues to ensure on-time launches.

Administrative Support

 - Track vehicle assignments, inspections, and maintenance logs.

 - Report and document incidents, driver notes, or missing items.

 - Support the operations team with ad-hoc fleet or logistics tasks.


WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - High school diploma or equivalent required.

 - Background in automotive service, fleet operations, or logistics strongly preferred.

 - Basic mechanical knowledge — comfortable changing oil, checking tires, and performing minor repairs.

 - Exceptional attention to detail and organizational skills.

 - Strong communication and teamwork, with the ability to work independently overnight.

 - Comfortable using mobile apps and learning new digital tools.

 - Must be able to lift 50 lbs, stand for extended periods, and work in a garage environment.

 - Valid driver’s license with a clean driving record required.

Compensation:

 - $24/hour

Job Title: Product Lead

Location: Los Angeles

Reporting to: Co-Founder

About 8Fleet:

8Fleet is building the foundational physical world infrastructure layer for autonomous and non-autonomous ridesharing — the scaffolding that makes mass AV adoption commercially viable. We are the fastest growing fleet operator in the world.

About the Role:

We are seeking a seasoned and versatile person to run Product. This is a rare, zero-to-one hire. You will sit at the intersection of product execution and growth strategy — embedded with engineering to ship product that moves the needle, and accountable for the metrics that prove it. This role demands someone who thrives in a fast-paced environment, can take ambiguous requirements and turn them into robust, high-quality, and scalable solutions, and can mentor peers in best practices. You will have an outsized influence on how this company scales.

What You'll Do:


PRODUCT EXECUTION

 - Own PRDs end-to-end — from problem definition through engineering delivery and post-launch measurement

 - Be the connective tissue between business goals and technical implementation; translate operator insights and partner requirements into crisp specs

 - Run sprint planning, manage the product backlog, and ruthlessly prioritize against growth impact

 - Define and instrument success metrics for every feature shipped; hold yourself and the team accountable to outcomes, not output

 - Use AI-native tools (Cursor, Claude, Replit, etc.) as a force-multiplier — prototype ideas yourself, generate test data, automate QA loops, and reduce back-and-forth with Eng


B2B PARTNERSHIPS & AV ECOSYSTEM

 - Build and deepen the product relationships with rideshare partners (Uber, Lyft, etc) and inbound AV OEM/operator partners

 - Identify integration opportunities, joint GTM motions, and exclusive data-sharing arrangements that create durable competitive moat

 - Own the partner product roadmap — surface their needs, translate them into internal priorities, and close the feedback loop


DRIVER & OPERATOR ACQUISITION AND RETENTION

 - Design and own the funnel from driver awareness to active fleet contributor — including onboarding, activation, and long-term retention loops

 - Develop and ship driver-facing product features that improve quality scores, earnings reliability, and satisfaction

 - Leverage data to identify drop-off points, experiment aggressively, and ship fast


PLATFORM GROWTH LOOPS & DATA

 - Build and own the growth model: identify the compounding loops in fleet scale, utilization, and partner economics that make 8Fleet's network effects defensible

 - Develop a proprietary data strategy — define what to capture, how to structure it, and how to turn it into fleet intelligence that competitors cannot replicate

 - Partner with engineering to ship internal tools that give operators real-time visibility and dynamic decision-making capability

 - Design and run structured growth experiments; synthesize learnings into a compounding playbook

What You'll Bring to the Table:

 - 5-9 years in product management or growth product, ideally at a hypergrowth company (mobility, logistics, marketplace, or gig economy strongly preferred)

 - Demonstrably AI-native: you use LLMs, automation, and AI tooling daily as productivity infrastructure — not as a novelty

 - Strong technical fluency; comfortable reading code, writing SQL, and in a room with senior engineers without losing credibility

 - Experience shipping B2B or platform product in addition to consumer-facing surfaces

 - Data-driven to your core — you build models before you make recommendations, and you know the difference between a metric and a KPI

 - Exceptional written clarity; your PRDs and strategy memos are the kind people forward

 - Background at Uber, Lyft, Via, Waymo, Cruise, or a fleet management software company

 - Experience in a founder-adjacent or "first product hire" role at an early-stage company

Job Title: Fleet Supervisor

Company: 8Fleet

Location: Miami, FL

About 8Fleet:

8Fleet is hiring! We are a leader in providing high-quality professional drivers, well-maintained vehicles, and consistent availability. Our commitment to excellence creates a premier driving experience for passengers and a rewarding workplace for our drivers.

About the Role:

We are seeking a highly motivated and results-oriented Fleet Supervisor to join our team. In this critical role, you will be responsible for the day-to-day operations of a designated shift, ensuring the smooth and efficient movement of vehicles and drivers. You will play a key role in optimizing resource allocation and maximizing driver/vehicle productivity.

Responsibilities:

 - Oversee daily shift operations: Monitor driver schedules, track vehicle locations, and respond to real-time issues such as traffic delays, accidents, or mechanical problems.

 - Manage driver assignments: Allocate drivers to appropriate routes, ensuring optimal utilization of resources and minimizing idle time.

 - Communicate effectively: Maintain clear and concise communication with drivers, operations management, and other company staff via phone, text and other communication channels.

 - Monitor driver performance: Track key performance indicators (KPIs) such as on-time delivery rates, fuel efficiency, and safety records.

 - Maintain accurate records: Document all shift activities, including driver assignments, vehicle movements, and any incidents.

 - Collaborate with other departments: Work closely with other groups to ensure seamless operations.

 - Identify and implement process improvements: Analyze operational data to identify areas for improvement and implement strategies to enhance efficiency and productivity.

 - Ensure compliance with all relevant regulations: Adhere to all safety and regulatory requirements, including DOT regulations and company policies.

Qualifications:

 - High school diploma or equivalent; Associate's or Bachelor's degree in Logistics, Transportation, or a related field preferred.

 - 2+ years of experience in a fast-paced transportation or logistics environment, with supervisory experience preferred.

 - Strong understanding of GPS tracking systems, dispatch software, and other relevant technologies.

 - Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to effectively communicate with diverse groups of people.

 - Strong problem-solving and decision-making skills with the ability to think quickly and decisively in high-pressure situations.

 - Proficiency in Gmail and the ability to pick up various apps and software as needed.

 - Ability to work flexible shifts, including weekends and holidays, especially Thursday-Sunday

Compensation:

 - $20-$24/hr

8Fleet is an Equal Opportunity Employer and values diversity at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, 1 gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.

Job Title: Head of Central Operations

About 8Fleet:

8Fleet is hiring! 8Fleet is a rapidly growing LA-based technology and logistics startup building the operational infrastructure for both autonomous and non-autonomous rideshare fleets. As we scale rapidly across markets, we’re looking for a world-class Head of Central Operations to build and lead the systems powering our operational backbone.

About the Role:

We are looking for a highly strategic and execution-oriented operator to lead Central Operations at 8Fleet. This role will own the operational systems, workflows, tooling, and teams that power driver growth, onboarding, launch operations, driver support, vehicle operations, insurance & claims, and fleet readiness across all markets.

You will be responsible for building scalable operational infrastructure while aggressively leveraging automation and AI to improve efficiency, reduce costs, and enable rapid growth. This is not a traditional operations management role — we are looking for someone obsessed with operational leverage, process design, and building systems that scale.

You will manage a lean, high-performing team while redesigning workflows so human effort is focused on high-value interactions and escalations, not repetitive manual work.

This role is ideal for someone who thrives in ambiguity, enjoys solving complex operational problems, and knows how to combine people, process, technology, and AI into a scalable operating model.

Key Responsibilities:



CENTRAL OPERATIONS LEADERSHIP

 - Own and scale Central Operations across all markets

 - Build systems and processes that support rapid operational growth

DRIVER GROWTH & ONBOARDING

 - Oversee driver acquisition operations and onboarding workflows

 - Build scalable onboarding systems leveraging automation and AI


DRIVER SUPPORT

 - Build a scalable driver support organization and operating model

 - Improve response times, resolution quality, and driver experience

 - Design support workflows that prioritize high-touch human interactions where they create the most value

 - Automate repetitive support workflows wherever possible


SAFETY, INSURANCE & CLAIMS

 - Oversee safety operations, claims processes, and insurance coordination

 - Reduce claims frequency, severity, and operational processing costs

 - Improve incident response and escalation management

 - Build reporting and operational visibility across safety metrics


VEHICLE OPERATIONS

 - Oversee vehicle infleeting, registrations, renewals, and operational readiness

LAUNCH & EXPANSION

AUTOMATION & AI

 - Identify and automate manual operational workflows across Central Operations

 - Redesign operational processes to maximize leverage from a lean team

 - Build operational dashboards, reporting, and KPI visibility


LEADERSHIP & CROSS-FUNCTIONAL PARTNERSHIP

 - Manage and develop a small high-performing team

 - Partner closely with Product, Engineering, Finance, People, and Market Operations

 - Establish operational metrics, accountability, and review cadences

 - Drive a culture of ownership, urgency, continuous improvement, and exec

source_jobs.title: Operations Coordinator - Miami
source_jobs.description:
Job Title: Operations Coordinator

Location: Hub - Miami

Reporting to: General Manager

About 8Fleet:

8Fleet is hiring! 8Fleet is a rapidly growing LA-based technology and logistics startup building the operational infrastructure for both non-autonomous and future autonomous rideshare fleets. With our anticipated growth, we’re searching for passionate team members to join and grow with our team. You’ll be involved in every aspect of the operations as we rapidly expand across Los Angeles and beyond!

About the Role:

We are looking for a highly organized and reliable Operations Coordinator to join our team. In this role, you'll be a key part of our daily operations, interfacing with drivers and ensuring our fleet of vehicles is ready for our drivers at the start of each shift and accounted for at the end. You'll be the point person for managing shifts, helping to maintain a smooth and efficient workflow for the entire team. This is a hands-on position that requires strong attention to detail and a proactive approach to problem-solving.

What You'll Do:

 - Supporting Drivers: Providing assistance and support to drivers, including troubleshooting issues, on-road coaching, and managing overall performance.

 - Scheduling Maintenance and Repairs: Coordinating routine maintenance, inspections, and repairs to ensure vehicles are in good working order.

 - Managing Inventory: Maintaining records of keys, vehicle equipment, and other supplies needed for fleet operations.

 - Monitoring Fuel Levels and Other Key Metrics: Tracking fuel consumption and other relevant data to optimize fleet performance.

 - Ensuring Compliance: Adhering to safety regulations, DOT regulations, and other relevant standards.

 - Optimizing Routes and Logistics: Working to improve routing, scheduling, and other aspects of fleet operations to maximize efficiency and minimize costs.

 - Working with External Vendors: Coordinating with mechanics, repair shops, and other vendors to ensure timely and cost-effective repairs.

 - Maintaining Records: Keeping accurate records of vehicle maintenance, repairs, and other relevant data.

 - Assisting with vehicle setup.

 - Process Improvement: Identifying and implementing process improvements to enhance fleet operations.

 - Scheduling and Time & Attendance Tracking: Monitoring, managing, editing, and approving timecards as well as creating weekly schedules for all supervisors and drivers.

Successful Candidates Will Bring:

 - 1-3 years of experience in startups or logistics.

 - Strong problem-solving skills and ability to multi-task under pressure.

 - Excellent communication, interpersonal, and leadership skills.

 - Proficiency in Microsoft Office a plus!

 - Bachelor's degree required.

 - Experience with ridesharing platforms (Uber, Lyft) is a strong plus.

 - Must be comfortable working early morning or late night hours

Compensation:

 - Pay: $60,000 – $70,000
   The pay listed is the salary range for this position. Any specific offer will vary based on the successful applicant’s education, experience, skills, abilities, geographic location, and alignment with market data.
   You may be eligible to participate in a Company incentive or bonus program.

Equal Opportunity Employer:

8Fleet is an equal opportunity employer and values diversity at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, 1 gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.

About 8Fleet:

8Fleet is hiring. 8Fleet is a rapidly growing LA based technology and logistics startup building the operational infrastructure for both autonomous and non autonomous rideshare fleets. With our anticipated growth, we’re searching for a People Tech & Analytics Leader to support our expanding multi location workforce and help build a high performance People function that scales with us.

About the Role:

We are looking for a People Tech & Analytics Leader who is responsible for the end-to-end strategy, development, optimization, and scaling of people technology, workforce analytics, and AI-enabled employee experience solutions. This role serves as a key partner to People, Product, Engineering, Operations, and Business Operations teams to build scalable systems, actionable insights, and predictive models that drive workforce effectiveness, compliance, operational efficiency, and business growth.

The ideal candidate combines expertise in HR technology, workforce analytics, AI adoption, and cross-functional program leadership to transform data into strategic workforce decisions and create a best-in-class employee experience.

Key Responsibilities:


PEOPLE SYSTEMS & TECHNOLOGY

 - Own the strategy, governance, and optimization of People systems and related technologies.

 - Drive system adoption, compliance, data integrity, and process efficiency.

 - Partner with Product and Engineering to automate workflows and enhance employee-facing tools.


WORKFORCE ANALYTICS & INSIGHTS

 - Build and scale workforce analytics capabilities that support business decision-making.

 - Develop predictive models focused on attrition, talent attraction, internal talent mobility, workforce planning, and labor risk.

 - Deliver dashboards, KPIs, and insights that enable proactive workforce decisions.


AI & EMPLOYEE EXPERIENCE

 - Lead AI strategy and adoption across the employee lifecycle.

 - Implement AI-driven solutions that improve employee support, ticketing, self-service, and operational efficiency.

 - Partner with People, Product, Engineering, Operations, and Business Operations teams to prioritize and deliver workforce technology initiatives.

 - Lead large-scale projects from design through implementation and adoption.

 - Influence stakeholders through data-driven recommendations and measurable business outcomes.

Qualifications:

 - Bachelor's degree in Human Resources, Information Systems, Data Analytics, Business, Computer Science, or related field.

 - 8+ years of experience in HR Technology, People Analytics, Workforce Planning, Business Intelligence, or related functions.

 - 5+ years leading enterprise-scale people systems, analytics, or workforce technology initiatives.

 - Experience partnering with Product, Engineering, and Operations organizations.

 - Strong understanding of HRIS, ATS, ticketing systems, workflow automation, and workforce management platforms.
